title: Fear Conditioning
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8plpn","text":"Behavioral assay to measure fear learning and memory in mice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7tlkm","text":"MedAssociates fear conditioning chambers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5j86u","text":"white floor inserts","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2ors","text":"black ceiling inserts","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6kjfm","text":"pepperminet and limonene spray","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Habituate mice to the room. Set up fear conditioning chambers as well as protocols (listed below) in VideoFreeze software.
Place individual mice in a lit fear conditioning apparatus scented with peppermint spray.

Protocol: 00:02:00 baseline followed by 5 tone-shock pairings where the shock follows the tone shock = 0.5mA, 00:00:01 is delivered after the end of the tone (75dB, 2kHz, 00:00:19 ) empty trace intervals between the shocks last 00:01:00
Goal: Measure the subjects' fear response to the environment where fear conditioning was received
After the protocol runs for each mouse, place the mouse back in the home cage and clean the fear conditioning chamber thoroughly.
24 hours after fear acquisition: Mice are placed in a lightened FC apparatus scented with peppermint spray.

Goal: Measure the subjects fear response to the CS, and ideally no other features ofthe training environment. The key is to alter the chamber so the subjects are exposed to a new set of environmental cues.

Fear conditioning acquisition % Component freezing for baseline, each tone (if used), shock, and interstimulus interval Graphs for each component should be graphed separately Average motion Context % Component freezing per minute and total Per minute and total will be on separate graphs Average motion per minute and total Cued %Component freezing for baseline, each tone, and interstimulus interval Graphs for each component should be graphed separately Average motion for baseline, each tone, and interstimulus interval
Contextual test is performed 24:00:00 after the fear acquisition test. Cued Test is performed at least 02:00:00 after the contextual test.
Mice are placed in a lightened FC apparatus which is altered by placing a smooth floor insert on shock grid and black triangles on top. Limonene spray is used, instead of peppermint scent.
Protocol: Neither cue nor shock are delivered during this test session (Duration 00:07:30 )
Protocol: Baseline: 2 min followed by five tone-cues No shock is delivered after the end of the tone (75dB, 2 kHz, 00:00:19 )
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
